description: Single-function Python code generation tasks (easy â†’ hard)

tasks.name: is_prime
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `is_prime(n: int) -> bool` that returns True if n is prime, False otherwise. Handle edge cases (n <= 1). No external libraries.

tasks.name: reverse_string
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `reverse_string(s: str) -> str` that returns the string reversed. One line body preferred.

tasks.name: celsius_to_fahrenheit
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `celsius_to_fahrenheit(c: float) -> float` that converts Celsius to Fahrenheit.

tasks.name: count_vowels
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `count_vowels(s: str) -> int` that counts the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u â€” case-insensitive) in the string.

tasks.name: find_duplicates
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `find_duplicates(lst: list) -> list` that returns a list of elements that appear more than once. Preserve order of first duplicate occurrence. No external libraries.

tasks.name: binary_search
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `binary_search(arr: list, target: int) -> int` that returns the index of target in a sorted array, or -1 if not found. Iterative implementation.

tasks.name: flatten_list
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `flatten_list(nested: list) -> list` that flattens a list of arbitrarily nested lists into a flat list. Example: [[1, [2, 3]], [4]] â†’ [1, 2, 3, 4].

tasks.name: two_sum
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `two_sum(nums: list, target: int) -> list` that returns the indices [i, j] of two numbers that add up to target. Each input has exactly one solution. O(n) solution preferred.

tasks.name: retry_decorator
tasks.prompt: Write a Python decorator `retry(max_attempts: int, exceptions: tuple = (Exception,))` that retries the decorated function up to max_attempts times on the specified exception types. Use functools.wraps. Raise the last exception if all attempts fail.

tasks.name: group_anagrams
tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `group_anagrams(words: list) -> list` that groups a list of strings into sublists of anagrams. Order of groups and words within groups does not matter. Example: ['eat','tea','tan','ate','nat','bat'] â†’ [['eat','tea','ate'],['tan','nat'],['bat']].
